title: Sara Blakely On The Origin Story Of Spanx: 'I Was Just A ...
text:
i am just a frustrated consumer
i had never worked in fashion retail i'd
never even taken a business class i grew
up on a beach in florida
and wanted to wear this certain outfit
that i hung in my closet and
everything showed underneath it and as a
woman you know i was like your clothing
is sort of the paint and if you don't
have the right canvas it affects the
painting and so a lot of women that i
knew struggled with what do you really
wear under these certain colors and
these certain fabrics what was the event
what was the first event i mean do you
remember that moment where you took the
scissors and just
i mean you could look at it as
destruction but i would look at it as
construction yeah i well i at the time i
was selling fax machines door-to-door
which can you imagine for seven years so
i graduated from college and i've been
doing that and i actually set an
intention for my life that was very you
know i had this moment where i had a
really bad day of selling fax machines
people kicked me out of the offices all
the time i always had my business cards
ripped up in front of my face when i was
cold calling and i pulled off the side
of the road this one day and i just said
i'm in the wrong movie
like call the director call the producer
this is not my life how did this happen
and i went home that night and i wrote
in my journal um what am i good at and
the one good thing in my good column was
sales
and i started asking like what what is
it about sales that you like sarah and
and i realized i liked offering
something to someone that either
improved their life or that they you
know that they needed and i wrote i want
to invent a product that i can sell to
millions of people that will make them
feel good
and then i asked for an idea to come to
me
and i waited two years
and so i only cut the feet out of my
pantyhose one time to go to a party i
put it on under white pants with strappy
great heels and it gave me a smooth look
in my clothes and you couldn't see any
undergarments and because i had set that
intention and asked so specifically for
my idea i was ready like anything that
crossed my path i was like are you my
idea like maybe you're the idea i've
asked for
so first of all do you still have that
original pair and do you still have the
scissors i don't have the scissors but i
do have the pair of pants
that inspired it all and they're
actually framed at the spanx
headquarters in atlanta georgia so
they're on the wall next to also my
lucky red backpack which is framed
because i started the company with a
lucky red backpack one of the very first
accounts i ever called on was neiman
marcus
and i didn't know how it all worked but
i called the local store in atlanta and
they kind of laughed at me and said
sarah we have a buying office and that's
in dallas i said okay well give me their
number and when i got the buyer on the
phone i said i'm sarah i've invented a
product that's going to change the way
your customers wear clothes and if you
give me 10 minutes of your time i will
fly there and show you
and she said okay i'll give you 10
minutes i jumped on a plane i went there
and right before i was going all my
friends said you can't bring that red
backpack like you're going to the neiman
marcus headquarters this thing is dirty
it's dingy from college i'm like but
it's good luck they're like sarah buy a
prada bag return it the next day like
whatever you need to do but you can't do
it and i you know i went ahead and did
it so the red backpack was with me every
step of the way too and that's framed at
spanx and i really do believe it's lucky
